examples.title: Point Processing Transformations
examples.description: Implements common point processing operations in Python using NumPy and OpenCV
examples.explanation: This code implements fundamental point processing operations including negative, log, power-law, contrast stretching, bit-plane slicing, and histogram equalization. Each function takes an input image and returns transformed output.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of pixels

examples.title: Point Processing in C
examples.description: Implements basic point processing operations in C using raw pixel arrays
examples.explanation: C implementation of point processing operations showing how to directly manipulate pixel arrays. Functions iterate through each pixel and apply the transformation formula.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is total number of pixels
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides input/output arrays

examples.title: General Point Processing Algorithm
examples.description: Generic algorithm framework for any point processing transformation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PointProcessing
BEGIN
    INPUT: Image I of size M×N with L gray levels
    OUTPUT: Transformed Image O
    
    FOR each pixel (x, y) in image I DO
        r ← I[x][y]      // Read input intensity
        s ← T(r)         // Apply transformation function
        O[x][y] ← s      // Store output intensity
    END FOR
    
    RETURN O
END

// Common Transformation Functions
FUNCTION Negative(r, L):
    RETURN (L - 1) - r

FUNCTION LogTransform(r, c):
    RETURN c × log(1 + r)

FUNCTION PowerLaw(r, γ, c):
    RETURN c × r^γ

FUNCTION ContrastStretch(r, r1, s1, r2, s2):
    IF r ≤ r1 THEN
        RETURN (s1 / r1) × r
    ELSE IF r ≤ r2 THEN
        RETURN s1 + ((s2 - s1) / (r2 - r1)) × (r - r1)
    ELSE
        RETURN s2 + ((L-1 - s2) / (L-1 - r2)) × (r - r2)
    END IF

// Histogram Equalization Algorithm
ALGORITHM HistogramEqualization
BEGIN
    INPUT: Image I
    
    // Step 1: Compute histogram
    FOR each gray level k from 0 to L-1 DO
        h[k] ← count of pixels with intensity k
    END FOR
    
    // Step 2: Compute probability distribution
    n ← M × N  // Total pixels
    FOR each gray level k DO
        p[k] ← h[k] / n
    END FOR
    
    // Step 3: Compute CDF
    CDF[0] ← p[0]
    FOR k from 1 to L-1 DO
        CDF[k] ← CDF[k-1] + p[k]
    END FOR
    
    // Step 4: Compute transformation
    FOR each gray level k DO
        T[k] ← (L-1) × CDF[k]
    END FOR
    
    // Step 5: Apply transformation
    FOR each pixel (x, y) DO
        r ← I[x][y]
        O[x][y] ← round(T[r])
    END FOR
    
    RETURN O
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode provides a general framework for point processing operations. The main algorithm iterates through each pixel applying transformation T to input intensity r. Histogram equalization shows CDF-based mapping for contrast enhancement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(L + M×N) where L is gray levels, M×N is image size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(L) for histogram and CDF arrays

examples.title: Point Processing in Java
examples.description: Java implementation using BufferedImage for point processing operations
examples.explanation: Java implementation demonstrating object-oriented approach to point processing. Uses BufferedImage and Raster for pixel access. Implements negative, power-law, and histogram equalization transformations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(M×N) where M×N is image dimensions
examples.spaceComplexity: O(256) for histogram and CDF arrays
